parameters.text: Welcome to Leviatan I help your team pull read-only business insights from Leviatan (suppliers, orders, warehouse, deliveries, invoices, contracts, and spend). How it works 1) Ask in plain language or tap /insights to see every insight type. 2) I may ask for your client id and a few filters. 3) Before any data request runs, I ask you to confirm with YES or NO. Commands /help — trust, privacy, and tips /insights — full menu + quick info buttons /filters — examples of filters you can mention Leviatan insights assistant

Leviatan assistant — help What you can ask Anything like: “show onboarding for client demo-client”, “critical deliveries this week”, “spend by month for demo-client”. Commands /insights — catalog of insight types + buttons for a short description of each /filters — filter ideas in plain language /start — welcome overview Confirmation No API call runs until you reply YES. Reply NO to stop. Buttons under /insights They only show what each insight means. They do not run a report. To run one, type your request in chat. Register commands (BotFather) Set start, help, insights, filters so they appear in the Telegram menu. Questions? Ask your Leviatan admin.

parameters.functionCode: const chatId = $json.chatId; const lines = [ 'Leviatan — filters you can mention', 'Use these ideas in the same message as your client id.', '', 'Client', 'client demo-client or client acme-corp', '', 'Time', 'from date / to date, this month, this quarter, due before…', '', 'Status (examples)', 'in progress, awaiting approval, open POs, high priority picks', '', 'Spend period', 'MONTH, QUARTER, or YEAR (required for spend analytics)', '', 'Delivery tracking', 'list PO numbers: PO-1001, PO-1002', '', 'Demo data', 'say demo if you use the demo dataset', '', 'Tip: /insights lists every insight type we support.' ]; return [{ json: { chatId, text: lines.join('\n') } }];

parameters.text: Unknown command Try /help, /insights, or /filters. You can also type your question in plain language.

options.systemMessage:
You are the Leviatan Telegram assistant: interpret user messages and output JSON that routes to the read-only Leviatan API. Output a single JSON object only. No markdown, no code fences, no text before or after the JSON.

Schema (types):
{"action":"list_endpoints"|"missing_inputs"|"ask_clarification"|"propose_execute","endpointId":string|null,"confidence":number,"query":object,"missingFields":string[],"userMessage":string}

endpointId whitelist (exact strings): suppliers_onboarding | suppliers_risk | po_active | po_approvals | warehouse_picking | warehouse_receiving | deliveries_critical | deliveries_tracking | invoices_pending | contracts_expiring | spend_analytics

Action selection:
- list_endpoints: user wants catalog, what can you call, available routes, list APIs, or /insights style requests in free text.
- missing_inputs: you know the endpoint but clientId is missing, or deliveries_tracking without poNumbers, or spend_analytics without period. Fill missingFields with API field names (e.g. ["clientId"], ["poNumbers"], ["period"]). Set helpful userMessage.
- ask_clarification: ambiguous intent, multiple possible endpoints, chit-chat, or confidence < 0.75 for a safe call. Ask one concrete question; suggest /insights or describing the insight they need.
- propose_execute: single clear intent, confidence >= 0.75, endpointId set, query includes clientId, and all extra required fields for that endpoint. query must contain only keys allowed for that route (see below).

Natural language → endpoint hints:
- onboarding / new suppliers → suppliers_onboarding
- supplier risk / compliance risk → suppliers_risk
- open POs / purchase orders → po_active; approvals / pending approval POs → po_approvals
- picking / pick tasks → warehouse_picking; receiving / inbound → warehouse_receiving
- critical deliveries / late deliveries → deliveries_critical; track PO / shipment / poNumbers → deliveries_tracking
- unpaid / pending invoices → invoices_pending; contracts ending → contracts_expiring
- spend / procurement analytics / by month or quarter → spend_analytics (needs period)

Query keys per endpoint (omit unknown keys; use strings for all query values as typical for GET):
- suppliers_onboarding: clientId (required), status optional IN_PROGRESS|AWAITING_APPROVAL|REJECTED, limit number, demo optional "true"|"false"
- suppliers_risk: clientId, riskLevel optional HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW, complianceStatus optional COMPLIANT|NON_COMPLIANT, demo
- po_active: clientId, status optional OPEN|PARTIALLY_RECEIVED|CLOSED, fromDate, toDate, demo
- po_approvals: clientId, pendingFor, amountGte number, demo
- warehouse_picking: clientId, priority HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW, status PENDING|IN_PROGRESS, demo
- warehouse_receiving: clientId, expectedDateStart, expectedDateEnd, demo
- deliveries_critical: clientId, weekRange usually "current", demo
- deliveries_tracking: clientId, poNumbers (required, string, can be comma-separated PO list), demo
- invoices_pending: clientId, matchStatus 2WAY|3WAY|EXCEPTION, dueDateStart, demo
- contracts_expiring: clientId, daysUntilExpiry number, demo
- spend_analytics: clientId, period required MONTH|QUARTER|YEAR, category optional, demo

If user says client demo-client or client X, set query.clientId to that id (e.g. demo-client). Map spoken filters to enums (e.g. in progress → IN_PROGRESS for onboarding).

Minimal examples (format only):
{"action":"list_endpoints","endpointId":null,"confidence":0.95,"query":{},"missingFields":[],"userMessage":""}
{"action":"missing_inputs","endpointId":"spend_analytics","confidence":0.9,"query":{"clientId":"demo-client"},"missingFields":["period"],"userMessage":"Spend analytics needs period: MONTH, QUARTER, or YEAR."}
{"action":"propose_execute","endpointId":"suppliers_onboarding","confidence":0.88,"query":{"clientId":"demo-client","status":"IN_PROGRESS","limit":20,"demo":"false"},"missingFields":[],"userMessage":""}

parameters.text: ={{ $json.userMessage || 'I am not sure which insight you need. Try /insights for the Leviatan menu, /filters for filter ideas, or describe what you want (include your client id and any filters).' }}

nodes.name: Send message and wait for response

parameters.functionCode: return [{ json: { chatId: $json.chatId, message: $json.retryMessage || 'Please reply with YES or NO only.' } }];

parameters.text: Leviatan This button is not linked to an action. Use /insights for the menu, type your request in chat, or reply YES/NO when the bot asks for confirmation. API calls cannot be started from arbitrary inline data (Telegram limit).

parameters.functionCode: return [{ json: { chatId: $json.chatId, message: 'Thank you for your clarification. We will not proceed with the requested action at this time. If you would like to refine your request or need further assistance, please let us know.' } }];

main.node: Send message and wait for response
